en.name: Whole blood specimen
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Whole blood specimen is a blood sample that has not been separated into its components (plasma, serum, cells). It is the source from which plasma and serum are derived, and is itself used directly for several assays — blood gas analysis, point-of-care glucose, lactate, haemoglobin (CO-oximetry), haematology (CBC), and molecular tests. The choice of anticoagulant in the collection tube influences which downstream tests can be performed: EDTA for haematology, lithium heparin for plasma chemistry on certain analysers, sodium fluoride / potassium oxalate for glucose preservation, citrate for coagulation studies. **OMOP CDM usage.** This concept set targets the `SPECIMEN.specimen_concept_id` field. In OMOP CDM v5.4, a MEASUREMENT row is linked to its SPECIMEN row via `measurement_event_id` (set to `specimen_id`) and `meas_event_field_concept_id` (a concept pointing to the SPECIMEN table); the older `FACT_RELATIONSHIP` table can also carry the link. In practice, many OMOP datasets do not materialise a SPECIMEN row and rely on the analyte-side LOINC to convey the specimen. This concept set is intended for datasets that explicitly model specimens, and encodes the **physical state and processing** of the sample, complementary to the anatomical-site specimen concepts (arterial / peripheral / venous blood). **Note: anticoagulant variants.** The SNOMED hierarchy under `Whole blood specimen` distinguishes the anticoagulant present in the collection tube. The current set captures all of them via the descendant relationship. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the SNOMED Specimen concept `Whole blood specimen`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to eight specimens: - *4122283 / 258580003 — Whole blood specimen (SNOMED)*: parent / unspecified anticoagulant. - *37155361 / 57921000052103 — Whole blood specimen with edetic acid (SNOMED)*: EDTA tube. - *44808076 / 838971000000104 — Whole blood specimen with heparin (SNOMED)* - *1447858 / 1351815005 — Whole blood specimen with lithium heparin (SNOMED)* - *1447859 / 1351816006 — Whole blood specimen with citrate (SNOMED)* - *44808077 / 838981000000102 — Whole blood specimen with fluoride oxalate (SNOMED)* - *1447857 / 1351813003 — Whole blood specimen with hirudin (SNOMED)* - *44808078 / 838991000000100 — Whole blood specimen with perchlorate (SNOMED)* ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Plasma / Serum specimens**: these are processed derivatives of whole blood, covered by their own SNOMED concepts. - **Cord, fetal blood specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Dried blood spot (DBS), blood culture bottles, smears**: out of scope for routine clinical biology. ## Mapping Notes - **Default mapping** when the source data records a whole-blood sample without specifying the anticoagulant: `4122283 / 258580003 — Whole blood specimen`. - **Source specifies an EDTA / heparin / citrate / fluoride / lithium heparin / hirudin tube**: map to the matching `... with X` LOINC code. - **Source records plasma or serum (i.e. the sample has been centrifuged)**: do **not** map. Use the dedicated plasma or serum concept set. - **Do not confuse with**: `Peripheral blood specimen` (anatomical site), `Plasma specimen` and `Serum specimen` (processed derivatives). ## References [1] SNOMED International. SNOMED CT — Whole blood specimen (258580003). Available via OHDSI Athena: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms/4122283
